info.name: CCS Profiling API
info.description:
Complete API collection for CCS Profiling System with authentication and all modules.

## Quick Start
1. Set the {{base_url}} variable to your server URL (default: http://localhost:3000)
2. Run the 'Login' request to authenticate
3. Access token is automatically saved and used for all subsequent requests

## Environment Variables
- base_url: http://localhost:3000 (local) or your production URL
- token: Auto-populated after login
- refreshToken: Auto-populated after login

## Authentication
All admin endpoints require Bearer token authentication. Login first to get your token.

exec: // Automatically add Authorization header for authenticated requests

item.name: Health Check
item.description: System health check endpoint - No authentication required

request.description: Check if the API server is running and healthy. Returns server status, timestamp, and environment.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints - JWT-based authentication with access and refresh tokens

exec: console.log('Access token saved to collection variables');

request.description: Get current authenticated user information. Requires valid access token.

exec: console.log('Tokens refreshed and saved to collection variables');

request.description: Change password for authenticated user. Requires valid access token and correct old password.
Logout endpoint. Currently returns success message. Token invalidation handled client-side.
Get paginated list of students. Supports search and filtering.
Get student details by UUID

Get complete student profile with all aggregated data. Returns student information along with skills, violations, affiliations, academic history, and enrollments in a single response.

Features:
- Aggregates data from 5 different modules
- Uses batch queries to prevent N+1 query problems
- Parallel data fetching for optimal performance
- Target response time: <500ms

Response includes:
- Student basic information
- Skills array with proficiency levels
- Violations array with resolution status
- Affiliations array with organization memberships
- Academic history array with grades and GPA data
- Enrollments array with current course registrations

Example response:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "student_id": "S-2026-0001",
    "first_name": "Alice",
    "last_name": "Williams",
    "email": "alice@example.com",
    "skills": [...],
    "violations": [...],
    "affiliations": [...],
    "academic_history": [...],
    "enrollments": [...]
  }
}
```
Create new student. Set create_user_account to true to create linked user account.
Update student information. All fields are optional.
Soft delete student (sets deleted_at timestamp)
Get paginated list of faculty members. Supports filtering by department.
Get faculty details by UUID
Create new faculty member. Set create_user_account to true to create linked user account.
Update faculty information. All fields are optional.
Soft delete faculty member (sets deleted_at timestamp)

query.description: Search by subject code or name

request.description: Get paginated list of instructions. Supports search and filtering by curriculum year.
Get instruction details by UUID

body.raw:
{
  "subject_code": "CS101",
  "subject_name": "Introduction to Computer Science",
  "description": "Fundamentals of computer science and programming",
  "credits": 3,
  "curriculum_year": "2025-2026"
}

request.description: Create new instruction/subject. Subject code must be unique within the curriculum year.
Update instruction information. All fields are optional.
Soft delete instruction (sets deleted_at timestamp)
Get paginated list of enrollments. Supports filtering by semester, academic year, and status.
Get all enrollments for a specific student
Get all enrollments for a specific instruction/subject
Create new enrollment. Prevents duplicate enrollments for the same student, instruction, semester, and academic year.
Update enrollment status. Valid statuses: enrolled, dropped, completed
Delete enrollment record

item.name: Academic History
item.description: Academic history management endpoints - Admin only

request.description: Get all academic history records for a specific student
Calculate and get GPA for a specific student based on their academic history
Create new academic history record for a student. Grade should be numeric (e.g., 1.0, 1.5, 2.0)
Update academic history record. All fields are optional.
Delete academic history record

item.name: Skills
item.description: Student skills management endpoints - Admin only

query.description: Filter by proficiency level (beginner, intermediate, advanced, expert)

request.description: Get paginated list of skill records. Supports filtering by student and proficiency level.
Get all skill records for a specific student
Create new skill record for a student. Proficiency levels: beginner, intermediate, advanced, expert
Update skill record. All fields are optional.
Delete skill record. Cascade deletes when student is deleted.

item.name: Violations
item.description: Student violations management endpoints - Admin only

query.description: Filter by resolution status (pending, resolved, dismissed)

request.description: Get paginated list of violation records. Supports filtering by student and resolution status.
Get all violation records for a specific student
Create new violation record for a student. Date format: YYYY-MM-DD
Update violation record. All fields are optional. Valid resolution statuses: pending, resolved, dismissed
Resolve a violation record. Sets resolution_status to 'resolved' and updates resolved_at timestamp. Resolution notes are optional.
Delete violation record. Cascade deletes when student is deleted.

item.name: Affiliations
item.description: Student affiliations management endpoints - Admin only

request.description: Get paginated list of affiliation records. Supports filtering by student and active status.
Get all affiliation records for a specific student
Create new affiliation record for a student. Date format: YYYY-MM-DD. Role is optional.
Update affiliation record. All fields are optional.
End an affiliation. Sets is_active to false and updates end_date. Date format: YYYY-MM-DD.
Delete affiliation record. Cascade deletes when student is deleted.

item.name: Events
item.description: Event management endpoints with participant tracking - Admin only

query.description: Filter by event type (seminar, workshop, defense, competition)

request.description:
Get paginated list of events. Supports search by name and filtering by type and date range. Each event includes participant count.

Event Types:
- seminar: Academic seminars and talks
- workshop: Hands-on workshops
- defense: Thesis/capstone defense presentations
- competition: Coding competitions and contests

Response includes participant_count for each event.
Get event details by UUID. Returns event information including participant count.

body.raw:
{
  "event_name": "Tech Conference 2026",
  "event_type": "seminar",
  "description": "Annual technology conference featuring industry experts",
  "event_date": "2026-04-15",
  "start_time": "09:00:00",
  "end_time": "17:00:00",
  "location": "Main Auditorium",
  "max_participants": 100
}

request.description:
Create new event. All fields except event_name, event_type, and event_date are optional.

Required fields:
- event_name: Name of the event (max 255 characters)
- event_type: One of: seminar, workshop, defense, competition
- event_date: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format

Optional fields:
- description: Event description
- start_time: Start time in HH:MM or HH:MM:SS format
- end_time: End time in HH:MM or HH:MM:SS format
- location: Event location (max 255 characters)
- max_participants: Maximum number of participants (positive integer)

The system will automatically track participant count and enforce max_participants limit when adding participants.
Update event information. All fields are optional. Only provided fields will be updated.
Soft delete event (sets deleted_at timestamp). The event will no longer appear in queries but data is preserved for audit purposes.

Get all participants for an event. Returns list of participants with their details.

Each participant includes:
- id: Participant record UUID
- event_id: Event UUID
- student_id or faculty_id: Participant identifier
- participant_name: Full name of participant
- participant_type: Either 'student' or 'faculty'
- participation_role: Role in the event (e.g., 'attendee', 'speaker', 'organizer')
- attendance_status: One of: registered, attended, absent, cancelled
- created_at: Registration timestamp

Add a participant to an event. Can add either a student or faculty member.

Required (one of):
- student_id: Student UUID (for student participants)
- faculty_id: Faculty UUID (for faculty participants)

Note: You must provide either student_id OR faculty_id, but not both.

Optional fields:
- participation_role: Role in the event (e.g., 'attendee', 'speaker', 'organizer', 'panelist')
- attendance_status: One of: registered (default), attended, absent, cancelled

Validation:
- Prevents duplicate registrations (same participant cannot register twice)
- Enforces max_participants limit if set on the event
- Returns 409 Conflict if participant already registered or capacity reached

Example for faculty participant:
```json
{
  "faculty_id": "faculty-uuid-here",
  "participation_role": "speaker",
  "attendance_status": "registered"
}
```

Remove a participant from an event. Permanently deletes the participant record.

Note: Use the participant record UUID (returned from GET participants or POST add participant), not the student_id or faculty_id.

Validation:
- Verifies participant belongs to the specified event
- Returns 404 if participant not found
- Returns 400 if participant doesn't belong to this event
Check for scheduling conflicts before creating a schedule

item.name: Research
item.description: Research management endpoints with authors and advisers - Admin only

request.description:
Get paginated list of research projects. Supports search and filtering.

Research Types:
- thesis: Graduate thesis research
- capstone: Undergraduate capstone projects
- publication: Published research papers

Status Values:
- ongoing: Research in progress
- completed: Research completed but not published
- published: Research completed and published

Each research includes:
- Basic research information
- Authors array with student details and author order
- Advisers array with faculty details and adviser roles
- Timestamps
Get research details by UUID. Returns complete research information including all authors and advisers.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Machine Learning Applications in Healthcare",
  "abstract": "A comprehensive study on applying machine learning techniques to healthcare diagnostics and patient care optimization.",
  "research_type": "thesis",
  "status": "ongoing",
  "start_date": "2026-01-15",
  "author_ids": ["student-uuid-1", "student-uuid-2"],
  "adviser_ids": ["faculty-uuid-1"]
}

request.description:
Create new research project with authors and advisers in a single transaction.

Required fields:
- title: Research title (max 500 characters)
- research_type: One of: thesis, capstone, publication
- author_ids: Array of student UUIDs (at least one required)
- adviser_ids: Array of faculty UUIDs (at least one required)

Optional fields:
- abstract: Research abstract/summary
- status: One of: ongoing (default), completed, published
- start_date: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- completion_date: Completion date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- publication_url: URL to published paper

Transaction Safety:
- All authors and advisers are added in a single database transaction
- If any step fails, the entire operation is rolled back
- Ensures data consistency

Update research information. All fields are optional. Only provided fields will be updated.

Note: To update authors or advisers, use the dedicated endpoints:
- POST /:id/authors to add authors
- DELETE /:id/authors/:studentId to remove authors
- POST /:id/advisers to add advisers
- DELETE /:id/advisers/:facultyId to remove advisers

Soft delete research (sets deleted_at timestamp). The research will no longer appear in queries but data is preserved for audit purposes.

Note: This does not delete the author and adviser relationships, but they won't be accessible through the API after soft delete.

Add an author to existing research.

Required fields:
- student_id: Student UUID
- author_order: Order of authorship (1 = first author, 2 = second author, etc.)

Validation:
- Prevents duplicate authors (same student cannot be added twice)
- Returns 409 Conflict if author already exists
- Verifies student exists before adding

Remove an author from research. Permanently deletes the author relationship.

Validation:
- Verifies author belongs to the specified research
- Returns 404 if author not found
- Returns 400 if author doesn't belong to this research

Add an adviser to existing research.

Required fields:
- faculty_id: Faculty UUID

Optional fields:
- adviser_role: Role description (e.g., 'primary adviser', 'co-adviser', 'panel member')

Validation:
- Prevents duplicate advisers (same faculty cannot be added twice)
- Returns 409 Conflict if adviser already exists
- Verifies faculty exists before adding

Remove an adviser from research. Permanently deletes the adviser relationship.

Validation:
- Verifies adviser belongs to the specified research
- Returns 404 if adviser not found
- Returns 400 if adviser doesn't belong to this research

item.name: Uploads
item.description: File upload management endpoints - Admin only

formdata.description: Entity type: student, faculty, research, event

request.description:
Upload a file with metadata. Supports multipart/form-data.

File Validation:
- Maximum file size: 10MB (10485760 bytes)
- Allowed file types:
  - Documents: PDF, Word (.doc, .docx), Excel (.xls, .xlsx), PowerPoint (.ppt, .pptx), Text (.txt), CSV
  - Images: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, SVG

Required fields:
- file: The file to upload (multipart file)
- entity_type: One of: student, faculty, research, event
- entity_id: UUID of the entity this file belongs to

File Storage:
- Files are organized by entity type, year, and month
- Naming convention: {timestamp}_{uuid}_{original_filename}
- Storage path is automatically generated and stored in database
- Supports both local filesystem and AWS S3 storage

Response includes:
- Upload metadata (id, file_name, original_name, file_type, file_size)
- Storage path for file retrieval
- Entity association details
- Timestamps

Get upload metadata by UUID. Returns file information including storage path, file type, size, and entity association.

Response includes:
- id: Upload UUID
- file_name: Stored filename with timestamp and UUID
- original_name: Original uploaded filename
- file_type: MIME type (e.g., 'application/pdf', 'image/jpeg')
- file_size: File size in bytes
- storage_path: Full path to file in storage
- entity_type: Associated entity type (student, faculty, research, event)
- entity_id: Associated entity UUID
- uploaded_by: User who uploaded the file (optional)
- created_at: Upload timestamp
- updated_at: Last update timestamp

variable.description: Entity type: student, faculty, research, event

request.description:
Get all files associated with a specific entity. Returns array of upload metadata.

Use cases:
- Get all documents uploaded for a student (transcripts, certificates, etc.)
- Get all files for a research project (papers, presentations, etc.)
- Get all attachments for an event (posters, schedules, etc.)
- Get all documents for a faculty member (CV, publications, etc.)

Path parameters:
- entityType: One of: student, faculty, research, event
- entityId: UUID of the entity

Response:
- Array of upload metadata objects
- Ordered by creation date (oldest first)
- Empty array if no files found

Delete a file and its database record. This is a permanent deletion.

Operation:
1. Deletes the physical file from storage (local or S3)
2. Deletes the database record
3. Both operations must succeed for the deletion to complete

Validation:
- Verifies upload exists before deletion
- Returns 404 if upload not found
- Returns error if file deletion fails

Note: This is a hard delete, not a soft delete. The file and metadata are permanently removed.

item.name: Audit Logs
item.description: Audit log endpoints for tracking system activities - Admin only

query.description: Filter by action type (create, update, delete)
Filter by entity type (student, faculty, research, etc.)

request.description:
Get paginated list of audit logs with date range filtering. Supports filtering by action type and entity type.

Audit logs track all system mutations including:
- Create operations: New records created
- Update operations: Record modifications with before/after states
- Delete operations: Record deletions with final state

Each audit log includes:
- id: Audit log UUID
- user_id: User who performed the action (optional)
- action_type: One of: create, update, delete
- entity_type: Type of entity affected (student, faculty, research, event, etc.)
- entity_id: UUID of the affected entity (optional)
- before_state: State before the operation (JSONB, null for create)
- after_state: State after the operation (JSONB, null for delete)
- ip_address: IP address of the request (optional)
- user_agent: Browser/client user agent (optional)
- created_at: Timestamp of the operation

Date Range Filtering:
- start_date: Include logs from this datetime onwards (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ)
- end_date: Include logs up to this datetime (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ)
- Both dates are optional
- Logs are ordered by created_at descending (newest first)

Requirement: 19.5 - Audit log retention for at least 1 year

Get audit log details by UUID. Returns complete audit log information including before/after states.

Use cases:
- Investigate specific system changes
- Review detailed state changes for compliance
- Track down when and how a record was modified
- Audit trail for security investigations

Response includes:
- Complete audit log record
- Full before_state and after_state JSONB objects
- User information (if available)
- Request metadata (IP address, user agent)
- Precise timestamp

Requirement: 19.5

Get all audit logs for a specific user. Returns paginated list of all actions performed by the user.

Use cases:
- Track all changes made by a specific admin
- User activity monitoring
- Compliance audits for specific users
- Investigate suspicious user activity

Supports:
- Pagination (page, limit)
- Date range filtering (start_date, end_date)
- Ordered by created_at descending (newest first)

Requirement: 19.5

variable.description: Entity type: student, faculty, research, event, instruction, enrollment, etc.

request.description:
Get complete audit trail for a specific entity. Returns all create, update, and delete operations for the entity.

Use cases:
- View complete history of changes to a student record
- Track all modifications to a research project
- Investigate when and how an entity was created/modified/deleted
- Compliance audits for specific records
- Data lineage tracking

Path parameters:
- entityType: Type of entity (student, faculty, research, event, instruction, enrollment, etc.)
- entityId: UUID of the entity

Supports:
- Pagination (page, limit)
- Date range filtering (start_date, end_date)
- Ordered by created_at ascending (oldest first) to show chronological history

Response shows:
- Creation event (action_type: create, before_state: null)
- All update events (action_type: update, both before_state and after_state)
- Deletion event if applicable (action_type: delete, after_state: null)

Requirement: 19.5

item.name: Dashboard
item.description: Dashboard metrics endpoints - Admin only. All metrics computed on-demand without database storage.

request.description:
Get complete dashboard metrics aggregating all statistics. Returns metrics within 1000ms.

Includes:
- Student statistics (total, by status, by program, by year level)
- Faculty statistics (total, by status, by department)
- Enrollment statistics (total, by status, by semester)
- Event statistics (total, by type, upcoming vs past)

All metrics are computed on-demand from existing database records. No metrics are stored in the database.

Response format:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "students": { ... },
    "faculty": { ... },
    "enrollments": { ... },
    "events": { ... },
    "generated_at": "2026-04-06T..."
  }
}
```

Requirements: 15.1, 15.2, 15.7

Get student statistics only. Calculates total student count from students table.

Includes:
- total_students: Total count of students (excluding soft-deleted)
- active_students: Count of active students
- inactive_students: Count of inactive students
- graduated_students: Count of graduated students
- students_by_program: Distribution by program (e.g., {"BS Computer Science": 50, "BS Information Technology": 30})
- students_by_year_level: Distribution by year level (e.g., {"1": 25, "2": 20, "3": 15, "4": 10})

All counts exclude soft-deleted records (deleted_at IS NULL).

Requirement: 15.3

Get faculty statistics only. Calculates total faculty count from faculty table.

Includes:
- total_faculty: Total count of faculty members (excluding soft-deleted)
- active_faculty: Count of active faculty
- inactive_faculty: Count of inactive faculty
- faculty_by_department: Distribution by department (e.g., {"Computer Science": 15, "Information Technology": 10})

All counts exclude soft-deleted records (deleted_at IS NULL).

Requirement: 15.4

Get enrollment statistics only. Calculates enrollment statistics from enrollments table.

Includes:
- total_enrollments: Total count of all enrollments
- enrollments_by_status: Distribution by status (e.g., {"enrolled": 100, "completed": 50, "dropped": 5})
- enrollments_by_semester: Distribution by semester and academic year (e.g., {"1st 2025-2026": 80, "2nd 2025-2026": 75})
- current_semester_enrollments: Count of enrollments in the most recent semester

Enrollments are not soft-deleted, so all records are included.

Requirement: 15.6

Get event statistics only. Calculates total event count from events table.

Includes:
- total_events: Total count of events (excluding soft-deleted)
- events_by_type: Distribution by event type (e.g., {"seminar": 20, "workshop": 15, "defense": 10, "competition": 5})
- upcoming_events: Count of events with event_date >= today
- past_events: Count of events with event_date < today

All counts exclude soft-deleted records (deleted_at IS NULL).
Upcoming/past classification based on event_date compared to current date.

Requirement: 15.5

item.name: Analytics
item.description: Analytics insights endpoints - Admin only. All analytics are computed on-demand from existing records without database storage.

request.description:
Get GPA distribution analytics. Computes average GPA from Academic_History_Record entries.

Returns:
- average_gpa: Average GPA across all students
- median_gpa: Median GPA value
- highest_gpa: Highest GPA recorded
- lowest_gpa: Lowest GPA recorded
- gpa_ranges: Distribution of GPAs in ranges (1.00-1.50, 1.51-2.00, etc.) with count and percentage
- total_students_with_grades: Number of students with academic history records

All analytics computed on-demand (no database storage).
Target response time: <2000ms

Requirements: 16.1, 16.2, 16.3, 16.7

Get skill distribution analytics. Calculates skill distribution from Skill_Record entries.

Returns:
- total_skills: Total count of all skill records
- unique_skills: Count of unique skill names
- top_skills: Top 10 most common skills with count and percentage
- proficiency_distribution: Distribution by proficiency level (beginner, intermediate, advanced, expert) with count and percentage
- students_with_skills: Number of students who have at least one skill

All analytics computed on-demand (no database storage).
Target response time: <2000ms

Requirements: 16.1, 16.2, 16.4, 16.7

Get violation trends analytics. Calculates violation trends from Violation_Record entries.

Returns:
- total_violations: Total count of all violations
- violations_by_type: Distribution by violation type with count and percentage
- violations_by_status: Distribution by resolution status (pending, resolved, dismissed) with count and percentage
- violations_by_month: Violation count by month for the last 12 months (format: YYYY-MM)
- students_with_violations: Number of students who have at least one violation
- average_violations_per_student: Average number of violations per student

All analytics computed on-demand (no database storage).
Target response time: <2000ms

Requirements: 16.1, 16.2, 16.5, 16.7

Get research output metrics. Calculates research output metrics from Research_Record entries.

Returns:
- total_research: Total count of research projects (excluding soft-deleted)
- research_by_type: Distribution by research type (thesis, capstone, publication) with count and percentage
- research_by_status: Distribution by status (ongoing, completed, published) with count and percentage
- completed_research: Count of completed research projects
- ongoing_research: Count of ongoing research projects
- published_research: Count of published research projects
- total_authors: Total count of all author relationships
- average_authors_per_research: Average number of authors per research project

All analytics computed on-demand (no database storage).
Excludes soft-deleted research records.
Target response time: <2000ms

Requirements: 16.1, 16.2, 16.6, 16.7

Get enrollment trends analytics. Calculates enrollment trends from Enrollment_Record entries.

Returns:
- total_enrollments: Total count of all enrollments
- enrollments_by_semester: Distribution by semester and academic year with count
- enrollments_by_status: Distribution by enrollment status (enrolled, dropped, completed) with count and percentage
- current_semester_enrollments: Count of enrollments in the most recent semester
- enrollment_growth_rate: Percentage growth rate compared to previous semester

All analytics computed on-demand (no database storage).
Target response time: <2000ms

Requirements: 16.1, 16.2, 16.7
